identity.resume_rule: new stream_session_id; preserve every logical execution identity

state_revision.static_type: StateRevision distinct from every opaque identifier type
state_revision.origin: assigned only by the authoritative interaction store
state_revision.increment: exactly once for every successful lifecycle mutation, including created-to-pending and each terminal transition; never for reads, rejected validation, authorization failures, or idempotent replay
state_revision.compare_and_swap: every remote mutation supplies expected_state_revision; mismatch rejects with input.stale_revision and performs zero writes, continuations, provider calls, or domain effects
state_revision.ordering: authenticate and authorize the principal before reading or comparing the revision
state_revision.overflow: reject further mutation as input.state_revision_exhausted without wrapping
state_revision.response: return the resulting revision on every authorized mutation and observation

sdk: all logical identity and trusted scope as opaque typed values

unicode.normalization: NFC before bounds, equality, and fingerprinting
unicode.bidi_and_control_characters: reject from identifiers and values; escape on presentation fields

inactivity_timeout_seconds.reset: only an authorized accepted resolution ends inactivity; rejected submissions never extend it
inactivity_timeout_seconds.active_use_pause: pause only while an authenticated interactive controller for this request holds the server-issued active-control lease
inactivity_timeout_seconds.trusted_activity_event: an authenticated controller_activity event carrying the request_id, controller_id, active-control lease nonce, and the next monotonic sequence number; answer submissions, validation failures, client-local input, and unsequenced heartbeats are never activity evidence
inactivity_timeout_seconds.resume: resume with the exact remaining inactivity budget at lease release, controller disconnect, or 30 seconds after the last accepted controller_activity event, whichever occurs first
inactivity_timeout_seconds.accounting: use the injected monotonic clock; accepted pause intervals are disjoint and do not reset or extend the remaining budget

required_wait_seconds.rule: no wait timeout; continuation TTL and explicit cancellation remain authoritative

durable_handoff_wait_seconds.scope: only the synchronous caller wait before returning a persisted input_required result; never an interaction deadline
durable_handoff_wait_seconds.prerequisite: every durable store, continuation resolver, and provider snapshot prerequisite is satisfied
durable_handoff_wait_seconds.exhaustion: keep the interaction pending and return detached_resumption_available=true without inventing an answer

text.empty_answer: allowed only when required is false

multiline.empty_answer: allowed only when required is false

multiple_selection.answer: ordered tuple of selected_choice(value) and at most one free_form_other(text)

defaults.automatic_application_requires: trusted host or caller policy with non-human provenance

state_transitions.timed_out_constraint: forbidden for required requests; permitted only after the declared advisory wait budget
state_transitions.illegal_behavior: leave pending, record validation failure, invoke zero continuations
state_transitions.alternate_principal_observation: resolved category and timestamp only; answer content withheld

outcome_to_model.declined: resume with explicit declined outcome when run exists
outcome_to_model.input_cancelled: resume with explicit cancelled outcome when containing run exists
outcome_to_model.timed_out: resume only for advisory declared policy; retain timeout provenance
outcome_to_model.unavailable: return explicit unavailable outcome when run exists
outcome_to_model.expired: terminate continuation and never start unrelated run
outcome_to_model.superseded: terminate stale branch and never resume it

execution.stream_rule: input_required terminates one transport segment but not the logical run
execution.suspension_boundary: after one complete provider request parses; before the next model call and before every dependent domain tool

reserved_call_batch_policy.reserved_with_domain_sibling: reject the entire provider call batch, execute zero siblings, create zero input requests
reserved_call_batch_policy.multiple_reserved_calls: reject the entire provider call batch, execute zero siblings, create zero input requests

execution.resolution_digest: canonical typed resolution plus provenance; semantic replay idempotent across transport keys
execution.transport_idempotency_conflict: same key with different content conflicts

record_fields.owner_id: non-empty worker identifier in claimed_pre_dispatch or dispatched_ambiguous; otherwise null
record_fields.lease_expires_at: monotonic deadline in claimed_pre_dispatch; otherwise null
record_fields.dispatch_id: opaque immutable logical dispatch identifier assigned before provider I/O
record_fields.provider_idempotency_key: stable key derived from continuation_id plus dispatch_id

continuation_claim_protocol.cas_rule: every mutation compares continuation_id, state_revision, and expected state in one durable transaction; success increments state_revision exactly once

lease.renewal: CAS by the same owner before expiry while still claimed_pre_dispatch
lease.reclaim: only after lease expiry and only when no dispatch_id or durable dispatch-start record exists

restart_actions.unclaimed: claim with CAS and begin one logical dispatch
restart_actions.claimed_pre_dispatch: same owner may renew; another worker waits until lease expiry, verifies no dispatch-start record, then CAS-reclaims through unclaimed
restart_actions.dispatched_ambiguous: do not steal or issue a new logical dispatch; query provider outcome by the stable idempotency key and replay only when the provider contract proves duplicate suppression
restart_actions.completed: return the persisted terminal result and perform zero provider calls
restart_actions.failed_safe_to_retry: CAS to claimed_pre_dispatch and retry with the same dispatch_id and provider idempotency key

provider_idempotency_proof.accepted_evidence: adapter-declared durable idempotency contract plus lookup by the exact provider_idempotency_key
provider_idempotency_proof.absence: remain dispatched_ambiguous and require operator reconciliation or expire the continuation without replay

exactly_once_accounting.continuation_dispatch: one logical dispatch_id may complete once
exactly_once_accounting.provider_call_count: count logical dispatch_id values; transport retries using the same provider idempotency key do not create another logical provider call
exactly_once_accounting.completion: persist provider result and dispatched_ambiguous->completed in one transaction before publishing completion

execution.ambiguous_dispatch_rule: never steal or replay unless the frozen provider-idempotency proof succeeds

provider_snapshot.serialized_form: strict canonical UTF-8 JSON with sorted object names, no duplicate names, no non-finite numbers, and no non-JSON values
provider_snapshot.revision_binding: provider_family, model_id, provider_config_revision, model_config_revision, and capability_revision must equal the trusted continuation definition reference

restoration.adapter: construct fresh adapter state from validated JSON; never deserialize a live SDK object
restoration.idempotency: one logical dispatch; any safe transport replay reuses the exact provider idempotency key
restoration.failure: resolve unavailable or expired according to lifecycle policy without starting an unrelated model call

execution.durable_snapshot_rule: unavailable for provider and model combinations unable to export and restore safely
execution.task_attempt_rule: suspension ends the execution segment, releases lease and worker, preserves attempt number and retry budget, and records resumed usage in a linked segment

containing_run_cancelled.result: cancelled with zero further model calls

cli_terminal_hangup_override.scope: attached-tty and piped-with-tty CLI execution modes only
cli_terminal_hangup_override.classification: containing-run cancellation initiated by loss of the owning interactive process
cli_terminal_hangup_override.result: exit 130 and zero further model calls even when an optional durable backend is configured
cli_terminal_hangup_override.precedence: overrides the generic attached-handler-loss branches for controlling-terminal disappearance

containing_state_mapping.rule: interaction state is always a separate field and never expands the containing task or flow state enum

public_failure_surfaces.description: CLI model using attached controlling terminal.
CLI model using piped initial input with a separate controlling terminal.
CLI model using fully headless durable controller.
CLI model using sessionless execution without retained continuation.
CLI agent using attached controlling terminal.
CLI agent using piped initial input with a separate controlling terminal.
CLI agent using fully headless durable controller.
CLI agent using sessionless execution without retained continuation.
CLI flow using attached controlling terminal.
CLI flow using piped initial input with a separate controlling terminal.
CLI flow using fully headless durable controller.
CLI flow using sessionless execution without retained continuation.
CLI task-direct using attached controlling terminal.
CLI task-direct using piped initial input with a separate controlling terminal.
CLI task-direct using fully headless durable controller.
CLI task-direct using sessionless execution without retained continuation.
CLI task-enqueue-worker using attached controlling terminal.
CLI task-enqueue-worker using piped initial input with a separate controlling terminal.
CLI task-enqueue-worker using fully headless durable controller.
CLI task-enqueue-worker using sessionless execution without retained continuation.
Public async SDK with an attached handler.
Headless SDK with trusted predeclared values.
Headless SDK with a durable external controller.
Serverless SDK host with durable handoff before process exit.
SDK host with no handler and no retained continuation.
Task target agent in direct mode.
Task target agent in queue mode.
Task target flow in direct mode.
Task target flow in queue mode.
Task target task in direct mode.
Task target task in queue mode.

Task target model in queue mode.
Task target callable in direct mode.
Task target callable in queue mode.
Task target tool in direct mode.
Task target tool in queue mode.

rows.attached_prerequisite: registered async resolver with live cancellation propagation
rows.durable_prerequisite: durable store plus registered continuation resolver and validated provider snapshot
rows.advertisement_rule: advertise only when either attached prerequisite or every durable prerequisite is satisfied
rows.snapshot_resumability: unvalidated and unavailable until the owning adapter activates
rows.fallback: explicit unavailable; never fabricate detached resumability

rows.attached_prerequisite: authenticated principal resolver, interaction authorizer, and registered async resolver
rows.durable_prerequisite: durable store, interaction authorizer, registered continuation resolver, and validated provider snapshot
rows.advertisement_rule: never advertises a model capability; operates only on an authorized opaque interaction reference
rows.snapshot_resumability: required for detached inspect, poll, resolve, and cancel
rows.fallback: HTTP 503 input.unavailable with no answer content

stale_schema_diagnostic.advertisement: forbidden until check exits 0 at the required schema head

task_target_support.incapable_rule: omit the reserved capability and return unavailable; recursive task, callable, model, and direct tool execution cannot suspend until a strict target runner owns continuation state

extension.absent: omit the reserved capability and reject forged reserved calls as unavailable

semantic_request_retrieval.authorization: apply error_status.remote_auth before reading request state or content

properties.shape: flat object whose values are primitive field schemas

requestedSchema.required: optional array of unique property names

elicitation.url_rule: sensitive and authentication flows use URL elicitation and never enter the task-input broker

tasks.stability: experimental feature in the pinned stable protocol
tasks.ttl_mapping: canonical continuation TTL seconds multiplied by 1000 without rounding
tasks.request_type_task_capability_absent: receiver MUST process request normally and ignore params.task augmentation

generic_receiver_task_requirement.omission_behavior: receiver MAY require task augmentation for a request type with declared support; omission MAY return -32600

tool_execution_task_support.absent: defaults to forbidden; attempted params.task SHOULD return -32601
tool_execution_task_support.optional: client MAY invoke normally or with params.task
tool_execution_task_support.required: client MUST invoke with params.task; omission MUST return -32601

tasks.tasks_list: optional, authenticated, authorized, paginated, and requestor-scoped
tasks.tasks_cancel: optional; transition to cancelled before the response and remain cancelled

mcp.multiline_degradation: form string with LF-preserving schema; unavailable when the peer cannot preserve newlines
mcp.other_degradation: separate tagged free-form string property outside every choice enum
mcp.error_ownership: duplicate creation, authorization, and state-conflict codes are Avalan policy rather than MCP core

a2a.transition_ownership: Avalan projection contract, not an A2A core transition law

follow_up.taskId: required and may infer the stored contextId
follow_up.contextId: optional; reject when explicitly different from stored contextId

AgentExtension.description: Avalan structured task-input request and resolution extension.

extension.unsupported_optional: ignore extension metadata and do not infer an answer from text

decline.payload: resolution with action=decline and no answers

cancel.payload: resolution with action=cancel and no answers

carrier_invariant.rejection: metadata without the matching Message.extensions URI and activated service header is ignored as unsupported extension data

accepted_answer_invariants: answers keys exactly equal the originating question_id set
text and multiline_text answers are bounded strings
single_selection answers contain exactly one selected_choice or free_form_other
multiple_selection answers contain no duplicate selected_choice value and at most one free_form_other
selected_choice values exist in the originating question choices
free_form_other is accepted only when the originating question allow_other is true

auth_required_isolation.handling: out-of-band authenticated URL or platform authorization flow

a2a.readable_fallback: human-readable request summary only; never sufficient to correlate or resolve without activated structured metadata

privacy.principal_resolver: authenticated and authorized remote principal resolver

submitted_secret_policy.classifier_interface: async trusted host policy classify_task_input(value, question_id, semantic_type, policy_revision) -> classification
submitted_secret_policy.authority: trusted host configuration selects and versions the classifier; renderers, providers, model text, and submitting principals cannot alter it
submitted_secret_policy.determinism: the same normalized value, question contract, and policy_revision must return the same decision and classification_id
submitted_secret_policy.false_positive_override: only an authorized host-policy administrator may install a new policy revision that returns allow; per-submission bypass flags and user confirmation are forbidden
submitted_secret_policy.classifier_failure: fail closed with input.secret_classification_unavailable, keep pending, persist no answer, and invoke zero continuations
submitted_secret_policy.diagnostics: emit only validation_code, classification_id, and policy_revision; never raw, normalized, hashed, truncated, or partially masked submitted content

privacy.redaction_rule: observer and error surfaces only; never silently rewrite an answer delivered to the model

literal_rendering.osc: strip control framing and render inert text
literal_rendering.bidi_controls: replace with visible escaped code points
literal_rendering.control_characters: replace with visible escaped code points

embedded_newlines.multiline_answer: normalize CRLF and CR to LF, then render literally

privacy.classification: same retention, encryption, access, and deletion policy as user conversation data

evaluation_order: validate authentication without loading the request
resolve trusted principal and tenant scope
perform scope-filtered lookup that treats absent and out-of-scope identically

not_found_or_out_of_scope.identity: byte-identical status, headers, and body for nonexistent and authenticated wrong-principal or wrong-tenant lookup
not_found_or_out_of_scope.timing: perform one scope-filtered store lookup; never perform an existence probe outside the authorized scope

authorized_forbidden_operation.scope: request is already known to be in the authenticated principal's scope, but that principal lacks permission for this operation

remote_auth.preauthorization_leak_rule: before authentication and scope authorization, never reveal existence, lifecycle state, revision, owner, expiry, or validation details

result.description: Exact result members are defined by the ordinary underlying MCP request method.

public_envelope_catalog_contract.each_entry: valid JSON Schema for the complete public envelope
public_envelope_catalog_contract.validation: every fixture-owned representative instance validates; missing required, extra, wrong constant, wrong type, and structured cross-field mutations must fail

a2a_input_required: result.task.metadata extension request_id equals result.task.status.message.metadata extension request_id
cross_field_invariants.a2a_resolution: result.task metadata extension request_id equals the authorized resolution request_id and task id/context equal the originating task
mcp: result._meta extension request_id equals the canonical broker request_id

repeated_requests.fingerprint: SHA-256 of required flag, NFC-normalized reason, ordered semantic questions, choice values, and constraints; excludes runtime IDs and display-only hints

activation.test_hosts: only explicit deterministic fixture hosts may advertise before atomic activation
activation.atomic_rule: activate only after every enabled consumer handles the canonical lifecycle
activation.rollback: stop new advertisement while retaining resolver capacity for already-pending requests

workload.arrival_release: release 10000 uniquely sequenced creation attempts through one barrier at the same logical start
workload.provider: local scripted provider with one response per accepted request

sampling.clock: running event loop time from the real monotonic clock
sampling.scheduler_lag: for each of 10000 arrivals, capture loop.time immediately before call_soon schedules a Future completion and again inside that callback; await the Future directly and subtract the two instants
sampling.resolution_dispatch: for each of 1024 accepted resolutions, measure from authenticated admission immediately before the broker queue put through durable state commit immediately after the queue consumer completes
sampling.manual_clock_scope: lifecycle deadlines and expiry tests only; never performance samples

percentile.comparison: convert the selected duration to milliseconds without rounding and require less than or equal to the threshold

overload.allocation: no continuation, request row, renderer work, or raw content persistence

capacity_budgets.measurement: barriers, queues, local peers, call_soon callbacks, and real monotonic loop time; no timing sleeps or network
